title: ericaceae
intro: The Ericaceae rank, also known as the heath family, is a taxonomic classification of flowering plants that includes approximately 4000 species of shrubs, trees, and herbaceous plants. This family is characterized by its diverse range of species, including heath, rhododendron, and blueberry, which are often found in acidic soils and cooler climates. Ericaceae plants are typically found in the Northern Hemisphere, with many species native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The family's unique characteristics and adaptations have allowed its members to thrive in a variety of habitats, from alpine regions to tropical forests.
